contraindications.clinicalRationale: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated in patients with a history of medullary thyroid cancer due to risk of thyroid C-cell tumors
contraindications.riskDescription: Risk of thyroid C-cell (medullary thyroid) tumors
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering medications without thyroid cancer risk
contraindications.sourceSnippet: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated for patients with a history of medullary thyroid cancer or with multiple endocrine neoplasia 2 (MEN-2)

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated in patients with multiple endocrine neoplasia 2 due to risk of thyroid C-cell tumors
contraindications.riskDescription: Risk of thyroid C-cell (medullary thyroid) tumors
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering medications without thyroid cancer risk
contraindications.sourceSnippet: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated for patients with a history of medullary thyroid cancer or with multiple endocrine neoplasia 2 (MEN-2)

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated in patients with a history of acute pancreatitis due to risk of recurrence
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering medications without pancreatitis risk
contraindications.sourceSnippet: GLP-1 RA are contraindicated for patients with a history of acute pancreatitis

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Exenatide is not recommended when creatinine clearance is less than 30 ml/min due to reduced clearance
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased drug exposure and potential toxicity
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other GLP-1 RA with no dose adjustment needed in CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Exenatide: Use with CrCl >30 ml/min, not recommended with CrCl <30 ml/min

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

conditionDescriptions: moderate to severe chronic kidney disease
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Exenatide extended-release has limited data for eGFR 15-45 ml/min per 1.73 m²
contraindications.riskDescription: Limited safety and efficacy data in moderate to severe CKD
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other GLP-1 RA with more data in CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Exenatide extended-release: Use with eGFR >45 ml/min per 1.73 m², limited data for eGFR 15-45

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Oral semaglutide is not recommended when eGFR is less than 15 ml/min per 1.73 m²
contraindications.riskDescription: Insufficient data on safety and efficacy in severe CKD
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider injectable semaglutide or other GLP-1 RA
contraindications.sourceSnippet: semaglutide (oral): No dosage adjustment, limited data for severe CKD, not recommended with eGFR <15 ml/min per 1.73 m²

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: GLP-1 RA should not be used in combination with dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitors
warnings.actionRequired: Do not co-administer GLP-1 RA with DPP-4 inhibitors; stop DPP-4 inhibitor before starting GLP-1 RA

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Risk of hypoglycemia is increased when GLP-1 RA are used in combination with sulfonylureas or insulin
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor for hypoglycemia; consider reducing sulfonylurea or insulin dose when starting GLP-1 RA

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Risk of adverse gastrointestinal symptoms including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea
warnings.actionRequired: Start with low dose and titrate slowly to minimize gastrointestinal side effects

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: GLP-1 RA should be avoided in patients at risk for thyroid C-cell (medullary thyroid) tumors
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients at risk for thyroid C-cell tumors
warnings.actionRequired: Screen for personal or family history of medullary thyroid cancer or MEN-2 before prescribing

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
